messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:

"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "It's essential to be proactive in managing and dealing with fake content on social media. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines and stories, especially those that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the sources of information by cross-checking them on reputable news websites or fact-checking platforms. 3) Use privacy settings to limit the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. 4) Report suspicious or fake content to the platform's moderators. 5) Practice critical thinking and media literacy by analyzing the credibility of sources and evaluating their bias. By following these steps, you can significantly reduce the amount of fake content on your social media platforms."
,"reasoning_summary": "The ocean personality traits of openness (34/moderate), conscientiousness (72/high), extraversion (69/high), agreeableness (13/low), and neuroticism (62/moderate) can affect how an 85-year-old woman deals with fake content on social media. Her anxious personality may make her more susceptible to believing false information, while her high conscientiousness and extraversion may encourage her to fact-check and report suspicious content. By understanding these traits, you can tailor your advice to her unique personality."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: It's essential to be proactive in managing and dealing with fake content on social media. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines and stories, especially those that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the sources of information by cross-checking them on reputable news websites or fact-checking platforms. 3) Use privacy settings to limit the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. 4) Report suspicious or fake content to the platform's moderators. 5) Practice critical thinking and media literacy by analyzing the credibility of sources and evaluating their bias. By following these steps, you can significantly reduce the amount of fake content on your social media platforms.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The ocean personality traits of openness (34/moderate), conscientiousness (72/high), extraversion (69/high), agreeableness (13/low), and neuroticism (62/moderate) can affect how an 85-year-old woman deals with fake content on social media. Her anxious personality may make her more susceptible to believing false information, while her high conscientiousness and extraversion may encourage her to fact-check and report suspicious content. By understanding these traits, you can tailor your advice to her unique personality.
